records.ety: [OE. ferme rent, lease, F. ferme, LL. firma, fr. L. firmus firm, fast, firmare to make firm or fast. See Firm, a. & n.]

defs: The rent of land, -- originally paid by reservation of part of its products.
The term or tenure of a lease of land for cultivation; a leasehold.
quotes: It is great willfulness in landlords to make any longer farms to their tenants.

defs: The land held under lease and by payment of rent for the purpose of cultivation.
Any tract of land devoted to agricultural purposes, under the management of a tenant or the owner.
notes: ☞ In English the ideas of a lease, a term, and a rent, continue to be in a great degree inseparable, even from the popular meaning of a farm, as they are entirely so from the legal sense.

defs: A district of country leased (or farmed) out for the collection of the revenues of government.
quotes: The province was devided into twelve farms.

defs: A lease of the imposts on particular goods; as, the sugar farm, the silk farm.
quotes: Whereas G. H. held the farm of sugars upon a rent of 10,000 marks per annum.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Farmed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Farming.]

defs: To lease or let for an equivalent, as land for a rent; to yield the use of to proceeds.
quotes: We are enforced to farm our royal realm.

defs: To give up to another, as an estate, a business, the revenue, etc., on condition of receiving in return a percentage of what it yields; as, to farm the taxes.
quotes: To farm their subjects and their duties toward these.

defs: To take at a certain rent or rate.
To devote (land) to agriculture; to cultivate, as land; to till, as a farm.
To engage in the business of tilling the soil; to labor as a farmer.
